findings.title: Engine-open failures before registration still leak local-sort state
findings.why: The new error defer only calls `CleanupAllLocalEngines`, but that helper can clean up engines only after they have been inserted into `em.engines`.
findings.risk_if_unchanged: A local-sort failure during engine setup can still leave behind an opened Pebble DB and partially initialized sort files, so retries do not get the full best-effort cleanup this change is trying to guarantee.
findings.evidence: In `RunSubtask`, the new defer unconditionally routes local-sort failures through `Backend.CleanupAllLocalEngines()`. That cleanup enumerates only `em.engines` entries. In `openEngine`, however, `openEngineDB` is called at line 236 and the engine is not registered until `LoadOrStore` at line 254, so errors from `os.RemoveAll`/`os.Mkdir` at lines 243-249 return before the engine is discoverable by cleanup.
findings.change_request: Please add rollback for pre-registration open failures, for example by closing and removing the just-opened engine inside `openEngine`'s early error paths instead of relying only on `CleanupAllLocalEngines`.
